title: celtis occidentalis var. pumila
intro: Celtis occidentalis var. pumila, also known as dwarf hackberry or sugarberry, is a small deciduous shrub or tree native to eastern North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating diarrhea, fever, and skin conditions due to its astringent and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Celtis occidentalis var. pumila include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves, bark, or roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
